Plaintiffs Notice Of Consolidated Motion And Consolidated Motion To Seal
Off Calendar Jun-23-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS MICHAEL TONG, RONALD TONG, MEI TONG, WILDLIFE MANAGEMENT, LLC, AND JAMES TONG, INC.'S Consolidated Motion To Seal.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs' "consolidated motion to seal" is granted. At this stage of the litigation, it has not been determined whether the to-be-sealed material is attorney-client privileged, but it is confidential at a minimum and sealable under California Rule of Court 2.550-51. (See Styles v. Mumbert (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 1163, 1167.) I find that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the records, (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the records, (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the records are not sealed, (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Defendants argue that sealing will prevent them from "introducing evidence," but that is not so in the pre-trial stage, as the court may view sealed material. Any sealing issues that remain if the case is tried can be addressed by the trial court.

Court to review proposed order submitted by plaintiffs' counsel.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
